title: Historic victory for Garden Route Badgers Colts
description: CRICKET NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers...
bodyText:
CRICKET NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers Colts team recorded their first-ever victory in a three-day match in the Cricket SA Colts competition when they defeated the Eastern Province Colts by four wickets at St George’s Park last weekend.


> Eastern Province, who batted first, was dismissed for 212 with Enathi Kitshini (3/58) the pick of the bowlers.


Despite a fine half-century by Breyton Plaatjies (51), the Badgers were bowled out for 187 - a deficit of 25 runs on the first innings.

Fine bowling performances by Elih Thorne (3/34), Grant Esau (3/29) and Sintu Majiza (2/34) were critical as the home side only managed to score 111 (all out) in their first innings.

The former SA U19 captain, Benni Hansen (53*), and Jonathan Bester (38) batted well to get the Badgers over the line (139/6) with four wickets in hand.

In the one-day match on Sunday, the Garden Route Badgers were dismissed for a modest 115, to which Eastern Province replied with 117/6 in 27 overs for a five-wicket victory.

Summarised scorecards

Three-day match:

Eastern Province: 1st innings 212 all out - Jordan Morris 63, Jean Upman 44, Jason Raubenheimer 29*, Reuben Wildeman 23, Enathi Kitshini 3/58, Elih Thorne 2/27, Sintu Majiza 2/42); and 2nd innings 111 all out - Divan Behrens 42, Akhanani Willem 29, Jordan Morris 23, Elih Thorne 3/34, Grant Esau 3/29, Sintu Majiza 2/34.

Garden Route Badgers: 1st innings 187 all out - Breyton Plaatjies 51, Benjamin Hansen 24, Grant Esau 24, Jordan Morris 2/43, Sihle Mginywa 2/25, Jason Raubenheimer 2/34, Esa Gangat 2/41; and 2nd innings 139/6 - Benjamin Hansen 53*, Jonathan Bester 38, Kyle Glennistor 2/17, Sihle Mginywa 2/40.

Garden Route Badgers won with 4 wickets.

One-day match:

Garden Route Badgers Colts: 115 all out - Mihle Dingiswayo 33, Enathi Kitshini 27, Jonathan Bester 20, Wezo Gqiba 3/6, Jason Raubenheimer 2/10, Sihle Mginywa 2/21. Eastern Province: 117/5 - Jason Raubenheimer 32, Wezo Gqiba 27, Elnathan Meiri 22*, Enathi Kitshini 2/39.

Eastern Province won with 5 wickets.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
